page: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8252C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, delivering 48 virtual CPUs across 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The dedicated CPU cores operate at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz, supported by 192.0 GB of DDR4 system memory running at 3 GHz, which translates to a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the m5zn.12xlarge demonstrates strong memory performance, achieving a top-tier rating in Passmark memory testing. Large-block uncached memory read bandwidth reaches 232.82 GB/sec, and write bandwidth is recorded at 97.66 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression is also highly efficient at 31.93 GB/sec. Conversely, multi-core CPU compute benchmarks, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, fall within the average range, and the instance exhibits weaker performance in LLM text generation tasks.

The instance is equipped with a high-capacity 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, making it highly suitable for network-intensive applications. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the combination of high-frequency dedicated compute cores and high-speed networking provides a robust platform for workloads that require rapid sequential processing without the overhead of local storage. Best-fit workloads include high-throughput web serving, database operations, and memory-bound applications that leverage its 100 Gbps network interface.
description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores) running at 4.5 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on its 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for external storage and communication. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory performance and strong large-block memory bandwidth, alongside average multi-core compute capabilities. This hardware profile is highly efficient for network-bound workloads, database operations, and applications requiring high single-core clock speeds.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs at 4.5 GHz, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for high-throughput network and memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for network-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency general-purpose instance featuring dedicated 4.5 GHz processors and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for demanding network-bound applications.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon processor with 48 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz
192 GB of high-speed DDR4 system memory
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Top-tier memory performance and strong large-block read/write bandwidth
Optimized for network-bound applications and database workloads
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
